message:
feat(abi): nativize all 68 XDR symbols with pure Rust RFC 4506 implementation

Replace the 68 dlsym-delegated XDR symbols in rpc_abi.rs with a
complete native Rust implementation of the XDR serialization protocol
per RFC 4506 / RFC 1832. This eliminates glibc call-through for the
entire XDR surface and several RPC utility symbols (portmapper, auth,
clnt error reporting).

The native implementation provides:
- ABI-compatible Xdr struct and XdrOps vtable matching glibc layout
- Three stream backends: Memory (xdrmem), Stdio (xdrstdio), Record/TCP
  (xdrrec) with fragment reassembly
- All primitive type serializers (bool, char, short, int, long, hyper,
  float, double, enum, and fixed-width int8..uint64 variants)
- Composite serializers (opaque, bytes, string, wrapstring, array,
  vector, pointer, reference, union, netobj)
- RPC message types (accepted_reply, rejected_reply, callhdr, replymsg,
  opaque_auth, pmap, des_block, keybuf, keystatus, netnamestr)
- Utility functions (xdr_void, xdr_free, xdr_sizeof)
- Portmapper client functions (pmap_getmaps, pmap_getport, pmap_rmtcall,
  pmap_set, pmap_unset)
- Auth DES and client error reporting stubs

Adds comprehensive integration test suite (xdr_abi_test.rs) covering
memory stream round-trips for all type serializers, composite types,
and stream lifecycle. Updates support_matrix.json to reflect the
GlibcCallThrough -> Implemented status transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
